_meta.source: ONS Crime Survey for England and Wales / Home Office
_meta.methodology: Crime Survey for England and Wales is a victimisation survey — it asks people about their experiences of crime rather than relying on police records, making it more consistent over time.

total_crime.label: Crime incidents per year
total_crime.note: CSEW estimate. Excludes fraud and computer misuse. Long-run trend is downward from 1990s peak.

charge_rate.label: Crimes resulting in a charge
charge_rate.note: Less than 6 in every 100 crimes recorded by police result in a suspect being charged. Rarely cited in political debate.

crown_court_backlog.label: Crown Court case backlog
crown_court_backlog.note: Cases waiting to be heard. Some defendants wait years for trial. Pre-pandemic level was ~37,000.
